lines.text: We're {{ELAPSED}} in and the Capitol is still standing, which I'll be honest, surprises nobody who's been paying attention for the last thirty years. They built that thing like they were expecting a siege, which, you know, they were. Cheers to foresight.
I've got a whiskey here and it's got a name, and the name is 'things I'm not supposed to say the other 364 days a year.' So I'm going to say them now. Tim, your levels good? Good. Here we go.
Caller: Marcus from South Side — you're on PRGE.

Caller: Marcus from South Side — 'Quinn, my question is why nobody talks about the fact that the government licenses this thing now? They make money on it.' Yeah, Marcus, because the people who profit are the people who enforce the law the other 364 days. That's not a conspiracy, that's a business model. We all know it. We just don't say it in daylight.

You want to know the saddest thing I know? Director Calloway — and I'm going to say his name because tonight I can — Calloway and I had lunch every Thursday in 2167. He used to tell me about his kids. Two daughters. Both in school. And then the scandal hit, and suddenly I'm persona non grata, and he's still sitting in the Capitol with three inches of blast glass between him and the people who put him there. I don't know if he thinks about Thursday lunches. I do.
The Vance Administration built this machine and then died of old age thinking it would run forever. And it does. It just doesn't run for them anymore. That's the real joke, isn't it?
Caller: Janet from Atwood — you there? Go ahead.

Caller: Janet from Atwood — 'I just wanted to say you sound tired.' Janet, I'm three whiskeys in and three hours into the worst night of the calendar. If I sound anything, it's functional. And barely.

The kill count as of 21:00 is 38 according to Tucker's last report. That's 19 people in the first hour and 19 in the second. That's actually below trend — Caroline mentioned it in the 20:00 block. The weather's helping. Cold fronts keep people inside. That's the hidden mercy of Wisconsin winters. We don't talk about that.

I'm going to pour another one and then I'm going to take one more call, and then Tim's going to cut me off because the night's long and I'm already sounding like I've got opinions. Which I do. Which I always do. Cheers, Madison.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide cumulative: 38 kills as of 21:00 (East Side accounting for 18; Capitol Square perimeter breach reports unconfirmed; 19 kills hour-two delta)
East Side holding strong at 18% of kill count YoY pace. Atwood / Schenk's Corners active all three hours.
Incoming: 340+ listener messages queued. Text backlog at capacity. Tim managing queue.
Caroline reports cold front arrival window now 00:30–01:30 (revised from earlier forecast). Wind gusts expected 15–22mph by midnight.
